# stack: af stack / unstack maintain the ADR-059 parent link in
# state.toml, and af sync refuses to run without one.
#
# Two fixture sessions: parent (feat/parent) and child (feat/child).
# The child's worktree path is relative ("wt/child") so it resolves
# under the testscript $WORK dir, where a real directory exists for the
# smart fake git (smgit/git) to "run" in. The smart fake reports a clean
# worktree, no origin remote, and merge-base == parent SHA, so the happy
# sync takes the already-up-to-date path without touching real git.
#
# Failure paths:
#   1. af stack without --parent.
#   2. af sync before any parent is linked.
#   3. af sync again after unstack removed the link.

env HOME=$WORK/home

chmod 755 smgit/git
env PATH=$WORK/smgit:$PATH

# ── Failure 1: stack requires --parent ────────────────────────────────────────
! exec af stack child
stderr '--parent required'

# ── Failure 2: sync before a parent is configured ─────────────────────────────
! exec af sync child
stderr 'sync requires Stack.ParentSession to be set'

# ── Link child onto parent ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
exec af stack child --parent parent
stdout 'stacked child onto parent'
grep 'parent_session = "parent"' $HOME/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/child/state.toml
grep 'linked_at = ' $HOME/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/child/state.toml

# ── Sync resolves the parent branch from the parent session's state ───────────
exec af sync child
stdout 'sync: feat/child already up to date with feat/parent'

# ── Unstack removes the link ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
exec af unstack child
stdout 'unstacked child'
grep 'parent_session = ""' $HOME/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/child/state.toml

# ── Failure 3: sync fails again once the link is gone ─────────────────────────
! exec af sync child
stderr 'sync requires Stack.ParentSession to be set'

# ── Initial file content ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- home/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/parent/state.toml --
schema_version = 1

[session]
id = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001"
name = "parent"
status = "active"
created_at = 2026-05-22T10:00:00Z

[worktree]
path = "wt/parent"
branch = "feat/parent"
base_branch = "main"

[execution]
tmux_session = "af-parent"

-- home/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/child/state.toml --
schema_version = 1

[session]
id = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002"
name = "child"
status = "active"
created_at = 2026-05-22T11:00:00Z

[worktree]
path = "wt/child"
branch = "feat/child"
base_branch = "main"

[execution]
tmux_session = "af-child"

-- wt/child/.keep --
placeholder so the child worktree directory exists for git's cwd

-- smgit/git --
#!/bin/sh
# Smart fake git for af sync integration tests (ADR-059).
#
# lifecycle.Sync issues (in the child worktree dir):
#   status --porcelain               -> empty output = clean worktree
#   config --get remote.origin.url   -> exit 1 = no origin, skip fetch
#   rev-parse HEAD                   -> child HEAD SHA
#   merge-base HEAD <parentRef>      -> parent SHA (equal below)
#   rev-parse <parentRef>            -> parent SHA
# merge-base == rev-parse(parent) makes Sync take the Rebased=false
# "already up to date" path, so no rebase is attempted.
cmd="$1"
shift
case "$cmd" in
  status)
    exit 0
    ;;
  config)
    exit 1
    ;;
  rev-parse)
    case "$1" in
      HEAD) echo 1111111111111111111111111111111111111111 ;;
      *)    echo 2222222222222222222222222222222222222222 ;;
    esac
    exit 0
    ;;
  merge-base)
    echo 2222222222222222222222222222222222222222
    exit 0
    ;;
  *)
    exit 0
    ;;
esac
